title: Was Rei really wrong
wikitext:
[]: Was Rei really wrong?             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 16, 2014 12:31 am Split from here - NemZ Bagheera wrote: without malice, Rei similarly did him a grave disservice by forcing him into the role she did.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 12:39 am NemZ wrote: I'm not following you here at all. She handed him the keys to anything he could possibly want, took steps to make sure he really did want it, and let him back out when he changed his mind even though he rejected her personally in the process. Where the hell is there a 'disservice' in that?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 16, 2014 1:36 am But she did try, he just was too wrapped up in himself to listen or understand. And she knew this, which is why she called in backup to try and get him sorted out, why she didn't immediately follow his first suggestion until he'd persisted against questioning, gave him another chance to back out once the process was started and he'd had a taste of it, and seemed perfectly fine letting the whole thing just drop and letting back out of the whole deal.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Sep 16, 2014 4:55 am Everyone is an unbearable arse in EOE. Perhaps it should be called Arsetrumentality. Godlike mother-monsters are not exempt from this.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 5:04 am It isn't that he was too wrapped up in himself to understand what she was offering. The problem is that she never clearly explained what that was, nor the actual, real life consequences of his decisions. Maybe she didn't know how to do that, I dunno. But either way it's clear Shinji didn't understand what was going on and she went ahead and changed the world on his behalf anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 16, 2014 6:14 am Bagheera wrote: It isn't that he was too wrapped up in himself to understand what she was offering. The problem is that she never clearly explained what that was, nor the actual, real life consequences of his decisions. Maybe she didn't know how to do that, I dunno. But either way it's clear Shinji didn't understand what was going on and she went ahead and changed the world on his behalf anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Sep 16, 2014 7:01 am NemZ wrote: I absolutely do think that Shinji meant it when he decided to kill the world and had he known it was really a possibility he'd have still said go for it.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Tue Sep 16, 2014 7:21 am With my EoE subtitles- Shinji: "Nobody wants me, so they can all just die. Nobody cares whether or not I exist. Nothing ever changes, so they can all die!" Keep in mind these lines follow after Shinji chokes Asuka too, so... I don't think he gave a shit about humanity's lives after killing one so close to him (metaphorically or whatever).

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Sep 16, 2014 7:33 am In EoE, "they can all die" is still passive - he has no interest in actually making everyone die. As you said, he didn't give a shit about humanity, and there's no more to it than that (after all, wanting them to die would actually be giving a shit!). Trimmed - NemZ

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Tue Sep 16, 2014 7:39 am pwhodges wrote: But even in EoE, "they can all die" is still passive - he has no interest in actually making everyone die. As you said, he didn't give a shit about humanity, and there's no more to it than that (after all, wanting them to die would actually be giving a shit!).

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Sep 16, 2014 8:01 am Well expressed; but equally it makes it even clearer that this situation does not match the end of Ha in a way that justifies the criticism of Shinji in that case.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 10:46 am NemZ wrote: I still disagree however because in that moment, in that frame of mind, I don't think explaining it any more clearly would have made a bit of difference. In fact some long-winded explanation might have just made him even MORE anxious to get it all over with.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Tue Sep 16, 2014 11:12 am It may be interesting to note Fuyutsuki's comment when Shinji became the Tree of Life. "Having obtained them both, EVA-01 has become like a God. And now it has been restored to the propagule of souls... the Tree of Life. Will it become the Ark to save humankind from the nothingness of Third Impact? Or the demon that destroys us all? Our future lies in the hands of Ikari's son. " To some extent, Shinji is the culprit, and less of it being Rei. To me, Rei is simply the genie. She has to obey her master, Shinji. Do we ever lay blame on the genie fulfilling the wish of his/her master? No, we blame the wish commander and then some sappy theme develops; "be careful of what you wish for" and "you never know how much something matters to you until it's gone." Not, "shame on the genie!"

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 11:23 am ThanatosII wrote: To some extent, Shinji is the culprit, and less of it being Rei. To me, Rei is simply the genie. She has to obey her master, Shinji. Do we ever lay blame on the genie fulfilling the wish of his/her master? No, we blame the wish commander and then some sappy theme develops; "be careful of what you wish for" and "you never know how much something matters to you until it's gone." Not, "shame on the genie!"

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Tue Sep 16, 2014 11:50 am Bagheera wrote: But with that said, what I'm talking about here is the fact that Rei didn't articulate matters in a way Shinji could understand before taking action. That's the real error here -- Shinji's intent, whatever it may be, and who's actually responsible for doing what was done are side issues.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 11:52 am But as I noted, that's a side issue. We're talking about Rei's actions and moral reasoning here, not Shinji's.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Tue Sep 16, 2014 12:01 pm Bagheera wrote: But as I noted, that's a side issue. We're talking about Rei's actions and moral reasoning here, not Shinji's.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 12:10 pm That's a matter of speculation, but yes, if you take that interpretation it effectively absolves Rei of responsibility. I don't think it's quite valid, though, since she's clearly prodding Shinji throughout the process. She's just doing a rather bad job of it.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Tue Sep 16, 2014 1:28 pm Bagheera wrote: That's a matter of speculation, but yes, if you take that interpretation it effectively absolves Rei of responsibility. I don't think it's quite valid, though, since she's clearly prodding Shinji throughout the process. She's just doing a rather bad job of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 1:33 pm ThanatosII wrote: From what I understand, it boils down to your claim that Rei was a disservice to Shinji for not explaining anything to him properly. However, can it really be called a disservice? Did Shinji ask, "Hey, if I truly want everyone dead, does that mean it will happen?" and then Rei refused to answer? That would be a disservice. Yet, no one demanded explanations. If Shinji wanted them, he got them (i.e. "What is this place?" "It's the primordial soup of life..." etc). Rei would be happy to explain anything to Shinji if he asked. There is no diservice. If Shinji wanted people dead, no problem. If Shinji questioned what Rei and Kaworu are, no problem, you have an answer. If Shinji wanted people to come back and return to reality, no problem, you got it.
summary:

Article: Was Rei really wrong
service to Shinji for not explaining anything to him properly. However, can it really be called a disservice? Did Shinji ask, "Hey, if I truly want everyone dead, does that mean it will happen?" and then Rei refused to answer? That would be a disservice. Yet, no one demanded explanations. If Shinji wanted them, he got them (i.e. "What is this place?" "It's the primordial soup of life..." etc). Rei would be happy to explain anything to Shinji if he asked. There is no diservice. If Shinji wanted people dead, no problem. If Shinji questioned what Rei and Kaworu are, no problem, you have an answer. If Shinji wanted people to come back and return to reality, no problem, you got it.
